skill.name: structlog
skill.description: Guides Python developers through structlog setup, JSON logging, context binding, processors, exception logging, and log testing.
skill.summary: Configure structured Python logging with structlog patterns for development, production, and tests.
skill.author: bossjones

security_audit.summary: All 36 static alerts were evaluated as false positives. The reviewed files contain structlog documentation, Python logging examples, and a generated marketplace report; no shell execution, cryptography misuse, reconnaissance, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection was found.

content.user_title: Add Structured Logging to Python Apps
content.value_statement: Python logs can be hard to search when important context is hidden in plain text. This skill helps configure structlog for structured events, JSON output, context binding, and testable logging.
actual_capabilities: Shows basic structlog logger setup for Python applications.
Demonstrates JSON logging configuration for structured production logs.
Explains context binding for request IDs, users, and related fields.
Provides standard library logging integration patterns.
Covers exception logging with structured traceback processors.
Includes pytest LogCapture examples for validating log output.
limitations: Does not install structlog or manage Python package versions.
Does not provide log shipping, retention, or hosted observability services.
Examples may need adaptation for each framework and deployment environment.
Does not automatically redact secrets from application log events.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Logging
use_cases.description: Configure JSON logs with consistent fields for services that feed log aggregation systems.

use_cases.title: Standardize Service Logs
use_cases.description: Create repeatable structlog patterns for request context, logger factories, and processor chains.

use_cases.title: Verify Logging Behavior
use_cases.description: Use LogCapture examples to assert that important events and context fields are emitted.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add basic structlog setup to a Python application with readable console output.

prompt_templates.title: Add Request Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to bind request_id and user_id so every log event includes that context.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Production JSON
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a structlog processor chain for JSON logs that integrates with Python standard logging.

prompt_templates.title: Test Logging Contracts
prompt_templates.prompt: Write pytest guidance for verifying structlog events with LogCapture and required context fields.

output_examples.input: Add structured logging for request tracking.
output: A request logger that binds request and user context.
Guidance for choosing console output in development and JSON in production.

output_examples.input: Make error logs easier to analyze.
output: A processor chain that includes log levels, timestamps, and structured exception data.
Advice on keeping sensitive values out of logged events.
A short explanation of where the configuration should run.

output_examples.input: Test that important log fields are present.
output: Assertions for event names and context fields.
Notes on isolating logging configuration between tests.

best_practices: Configure structlog once during application startup.
Bind request context early and reuse the bound logger through the request lifecycle.
Use console rendering for local debugging and JSON rendering for production pipelines.
anti_patterns: Logging passwords, tokens, session cookies, or personal data in structured fields.
Reconfiguring structlog inside frequently called request or worker functions.
Using development console renderers for high-volume production workloads.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps configure structlog patterns for structured Python logging, context binding, JSON output, and tests.

faq.question: Does it execute code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples that a developer can adapt manually.

faq.question: Can it work with Python standard logging?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples that connect structlog with the Python logging module.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production logs?
faq.answer: Yes, when adapted carefully with JSON rendering, stable fields, and secret redaction controls.

faq.question: Does it manage log storage?
faq.answer: No. It does not configure retention, dashboards, transport, or hosted logging platforms.

faq.question: Can it help test logging behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes LogCapture patterns for asserting emitted events and context fields.
